- Rajkot: 3059 Hospitalised With Stomach Pain | Summer Heat Triggers Gastro
Summer heat triggers sharp rise in gastro cases:Over 3,000 patients hospitalised in Rajkot with severe stomach pain complaints
- Copy link
As temperatures across the state have crossed 43°C, cases of stomach-related illnesses are rising rapidly. Since March, the heat has intensified continuously, and from the beginning of May, temperatures have remained above 42°C in several cities, including Rajkot.
Along with dehydration and heatstroke, hospitals are also witnessing a sharp increase in patients suffering from stomach pain and gastro-related illnesses.
According to official data, between January and April 2026, as many as 3,059 patients in Rajkot were admitted to hospitals through the ‘108’ ambulance service due to severe stomach pain. Officials believe many more patients may have reached hospitals on their own for treatment.
Poor eating habits increasing gastro cases: Dr Praful Kamani
Former Rajkot Indian Medical Association (IMA) president and noted gastroenterologist Dr Praful Kamani said the combination of extreme heat and the ongoing wedding season has led to people neglecting their diet and hygiene.
Speaking to Divya Bhaskar, he said that over the last two months, hospitals have seen a significant rise in patients complaining of stomach pain, diarrhoea, vomiting, bloating and indigestion.
Check hygiene before consuming food or cold drinks
